data.title: Strict /code_review of full repo (visual-layer focus) — pre-DESIGN.md merge audit

data.to: Run the /code_review skill across the full repo to audit visual layer (src/index.css, src/data/houses.ts, src/components/**, src/pages/**) plus general code health. Findings will inform a potential DESIGN.md v0.2 amendment and may spawn follow-up cleanup tasks (e.g., the stray #faf8f5 in Home.tsx:25, stale #f7f6f2 comment in index.css:46, possibly-dead FractalObject.tsx). Output: structured findings file at .lattice/notes/<task_id>-code-review.md. No code changes in this task — findings only.

data.to:
**Step 1 of 3** in the DESIGN.md workstream.

Run the `/code_review` skill across the full repo with emphasis on the visual layer. The objective is to produce a structured findings document that becomes the input for FRAC-22-DESIGN-GAP (gap analysis) and ultimately FRAC-19 (DESIGN.md authoring).

## Scope — what to audit

- **Design style overall**: aesthetic conventions, editorial tone, mobile-first conformance against the PRD baseline (375px)
- **Color palette in actual use**: every hex/HSL value in `src/index.css`, `src/data/houses.ts`, `src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx`, hardcoded `bg-[#...]` and `text-[#...]` in `src/pages/**` and `src/components/**`. Note inconsistencies, duplications, near-duplicates (e.g., `#f8f6f0` vs `#faf8f5`), and any tokens defined but never used.
- **Layout strategy**: grid/container patterns, max-widths, breakpoints, mobile-first conformance, horizontal padding conventions (`px-[4.5%]` vs `px-6`), vertical rhythm (`py-40`, `py-60`), section structures.
- **Fonts and how they are used**: Fraunces, Jacquard 24, JetBrains Mono. The uppercase + italic auto-application via `h1..h6` and `.font-serif` rules. The `[style*="Jacquard"]` escape hatch. Inconsistent usage of `font-mono` vs `font-sans` since they resolve to the same family.
- **Buttons**: every button/CTA variant, including shadcn defaults from `components.json`, the `cta-outline-link` inline pattern, the `link-underline` hover utility. Inconsistencies in size, padding, hover/focus states.
- **Shaders / textures / effects**: the body noise SVG, hero text-shadow, link-underline cubic-bezier, `animate-blink` keyframes, any other `@keyframes`, the R3F octahedron in `src/components/three/**`, banner clip-paths, Mandelbrot stamps, FadeIn / Framer Motion easings.
- **General code health (secondary)**: dead code candidates (e.g., `FractalObject.tsx`), unused tokens, accessibility issues (contrast ratios, focus indicators, prefers-reduced-motion handling).

## Deliverable

A structured findings document at `.lattice/notes/<task_id>-code-review.md` covering each scope area. Each finding tagged severity (critical / warning / info) and category (palette / layout / fonts / components / motion / a11y / health).

## NOT in scope
- No source files modified — this is a read-only audit.
- No DESIGN.md authoring (that's FRAC-19).
- No gap analysis vs design.md spec (that's a separate downstream task).
- Do NOT resolve FRAC-20 (octahedron tint issue at needs_human).

## Blocks
FRAC-22-DESIGN-GAP (the gap-analysis task), which in turn blocks FRAC-19 (DESIGN.md authoring).

data.to:
**Step 1 of 3** in the DESIGN.md workstream.

Run a custom 6-agent audit of the Fractal NYC visual layer, using the `team_three_review` skill's architecture (Claude × Codex × Gemini, each running standard + critical) but with a **whole-state audit prompt** instead of a branch-diff prompt.

## Why adapted, not canonical
The canonical `team_three_review` (`/Users/fractalos/Dev/Agentic-Engineering/commands/team_three_review.md`) reviews `git diff origin/dev...HEAD`. The current branch has only ~5 lines of relevant diff. We want a whole-site audit, not a diff review.

## Architecture
Reuse team_three_review's Phases 3–8 (context doc, prompt build, 6-agent launch, synthesis report) but replace the diff in the context with a **visual-layer audit brief** that points each agent at specific files and asks for findings.

## Project-specific adjustments
- Base: `master` (not `origin/dev`)
- Package manager: `pnpm` (not `npm`)
- Test commands: `pnpm type-check`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test` (verify these exist before running)
- Workspace temp dir: `notes/.tmp/`

## Audit scope (the brief each agent gets)
1. **Design style overall** — aesthetic conventions, editorial tone, mobile-first conformance against 375px PRD baseline
2. **Color palette in actual use** — every hex/HSL value in `src/index.css`, `src/data/houses.ts`, `src/components/three/OctahedronHero.tsx`, hardcoded `bg-[#...]`/`text-[#...]` in `src/pages/**` and `src/components/**`. Inconsistencies, duplicates, near-duplicates (`#f8f6f0` vs `#faf8f5`), unused tokens
3. **Layout strategy** — grid/container patterns, max-widths, breakpoints, mobile-first conformance, horizontal padding (`px-[4.5%]` vs `px-6`), vertical rhythm (`py-40`, `py-60`)
4. **Fonts and usage** — Fraunces / Jacquard 24 / JetBrains Mono. Uppercase + italic auto-application via `h1..h6` and `.font-serif`. The `[style*="Jacquard"]` escape. `font-mono` vs `font-sans` resolving to same family
5. **Buttons** — every variant: shadcn defaults from `components.json`, the `cta-outline-link` inline pattern, the `link-underline` hover utility. Size/padding/hover/focus consistency
6. **Shaders / textures / effects** — body noise SVG, hero text-shadow, link-underline cubic-bezier, `animate-blink`, other `@keyframes`, R3F octahedron, banner clip-paths, Mandelbrot stamps, FadeIn / Framer Motion easings
7. **General code health (secondary)** — dead code (e.g., `FractalObject.tsx`), unused tokens, a11y (contrast, focus, prefers-reduced-motion)

## Deliverables
- Per-agent reviews in `notes/CR-FRAC-21-{Model}-{Standard|Critical}-{timestamp}.md` (6 files)
- Synthesized report at `notes/.lattice/notes/FRAC-21-team-review-{timestamp}.md` with: executive summary, high-confidence issues (3+ agents), standard consensus, critical findings, contradictions, blockers/important/consider, action items

## NOT in scope
- No source files modified — read-only audit
- No DESIGN.md authoring (that's FRAC-19)
- No gap analysis vs design.md spec (that's FRAC-23)
- Do NOT resolve FRAC-20

## Prerequisites
- `codex` CLI installed and authenticated
- `gemini` CLI installed and authenticated (gemini-3-pro-preview)
- Claude CLI available with `--dangerously-skip-permissions`

## Cost note
Launches 6 cloud agents in parallel (Opus × 2 + Codex × 2 + Gemini × 2). Non-trivial cost. Per global CLAUDE.md token-expensive tools convention, user-explicit invocation = implicit confirmation, but worth flagging.

## Blocks
FRAC-23 (gap analysis), which in turn blocks FRAC-19 (DESIGN.md authoring).

data.reason: Read-only audit producing a findings doc; skipping plan gate.

provenance.reason: Read-only audit producing a findings doc; skipping plan gate.

data.body: 6-agent audit complete. 4 of 6 reviews succeeded (Claude × 2, Codex × 2); both Gemini agents exhausted gemini-3-pro-preview capacity after 10 retries. Synthesis at .lattice/notes/FRAC-21-team-review-20260605-1551.md. Consensus across all 4: (H1) house palette has no single source of truth — 4-5 conflicting palettes per house with hue-family conflicts on Campus (blue→green) and School (navy→red); (H2) shadcn Button is unused by every product page AND references undefined Replit CSS vars — silent no-op hover/active/outline; (H3) prefers-reduced-motion respected in exactly one place — FadeIn + octahedron motion + Navbar scroll ignore it; (H4) FractalObject.tsx is dead code with stale palette and Political Club still visible (contradicts FRAC-161); (H5) cream surface has 3+ near-duplicates, canonical #f8f6f0 not actually used in the hero. Strong recommendation: address H1, H4, H5 (palette SoT + dead code + cream normalize) as upstream cleanup tasks BEFORE FRAC-19 (DESIGN.md), otherwise we canonicalize one of four conflicting palettes.
